zh.Chelmsford, Essex, England: 英國艾可傑爾姆斯福德
zh.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell是Sir Hugh de Tyrell和Jane Flambert的兒子，於1280年出生在英國艾可傑爾姆斯福德。他於大約1306年與Margaret Heron在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1343年在英國英格蘭艾可享年64歲。

zh-hans.Chelmsford, Essex, England: 英国艾可傑爾姆斯福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell是Sir Hugh de Tyrell和Jane Flambert的儿子，于1280年出生在英国艾可傑爾姆斯福德。他于大约1306年与Margaret Heron在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1343年在英国英格兰艾可享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: Tyrell
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Tyrrell  .

variants.name: Tyrrell
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): perhaps a nickname from a derivative of Old French tirer ‘to pull’, in the same sense as French Tirand ‘one who pulls on the reins’, hence ‘obstinate, stubborn’. This name may have become confused with Turrell . A branch of this Anglo-Norman family has been in Ireland since the 12th century. Its name has been Gaelicized as Tirial.
English: habitational name from Tirril (Cumbria), which derives from Old Norse tyri ‘resinous wood’ + erg ‘shieling’.

nameMeanings.name: James
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of the name borne in the New Testament by two of Christ's disciples, James son of Zebedee and James son of Alphaeus. This form comes from Late Latin Iacomus, a variant of Iacobus, Latin form of Greek Iakobos. This is the same name as Old Testament Jacob (Hebrew Yaakov), but for many centuries now they have been thought of in the English-speaking world as two distinct names. In Britain, James is a royal name that from the beginning of the 15th century onwards was associated particularly with the Scottish house of Stewart: James I of Scotland ( 1394–1437 ; ruled 1424–37 ) was a patron of the arts and a noted poet, as well as an energetic ruler. King James VI of Scotland ( 1566–1625 ; reigned 1567–1625 ) succeeded to the throne of England in 1603 . His grandson, James II of England ( 1633–1701 ; reigned 1685–8 ) was a Roman Catholic, deposed in 1688 in favour of his Protestant daughter Mary and her husband William of Orange. From then on he, his son (also called James), and his grandson Charles (‘Bonnie Prince Charlie’) made various unsuccessful attempts to recover the English throne. Their supporters were known as Jacobites (from Latin Iacobus), and the name James became for a while particularly associated with Roman Catholicism on the one hand, and Highland opposition to the English government on the other. Nevertheless, it has since become one of the most perennially popular boys' names.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell was born in 1280, in Chelmsford, Essex, England as the son of Sir Hugh de Tyrell and Jane Flambert. He married Margaret Heron about 1306, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 1343, in Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell est né en 1280, à Chelmsford, Essex, Angleterre, fils de Sir Hugh de Tyrell et Jane Flambert. Il a épousé Margaret Heron environ 1306, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1343, à Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell wurde 1280 in Chelmsford, Essex, England, als Sohn von Sir Hugh de Tyrell und Jane Flambert geboren. Er hat um 1306 in Essex, England Margaret Heron geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1343 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Essex, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell è nato il nel 1280 nel Chelmsford, Essex, England come figlio di Sir Hugh de Tyrell e Jane Flambert. Lui sposato Margaret Heron circa 1306, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 1343, nel Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.Buttsbury, Essex, England: イギリスエセックスブッツブリー
ja.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrellは1280年にイギリスエセックスチェルムスフォードでSir Hugh de TyrellとJane Flambertの息子として生まれた。約1306年にイギリスエセックスでMargaret Heronと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1343年にイギリスイギリスエセックスで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell(은)는 1280년 영국 에세스 헬스퍼드에서 Sir Hugh de Tyrell(와)과 Jane Flambert의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1306년 영국 에세스에서 Margaret Heron(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1343년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell nasceu em 1280, em Chelmsford, Essex, Inglaterra filho de Sir Hugh de Tyrell e Jane Flambert. Ele casou-se com Margaret Heron aproximadamente 1306, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1343, em Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell родился в 1280 г. в местности челмсфорд, эссекс, Англия. Его родителей звали Sir Hugh de Tyrell и Jane Flambert. Он женился на женщине по имени Margaret Heron около 1306 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Он умер около 1343 г. в местности эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 64 лет.

sm.von um 1305 bis 1318: mai le e tusa o le 1305 e o'o I le 1318

es.lifeSummary: Sir James Tyrell nació en 1280, en Chelmsford, Essex, Inglaterra como hijo de Sir Hugh de Tyrell y Jane Flambert. Se casó con Margaret Heron aproximadamente 1306, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 1343, en Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 64 años.
